title: Lathyrus collinus
taxonomy: Lathyrus collinus is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Lathyrus collinus is classified as a perennial herbaceous plant. This species is further classified within the tribe Fabaceae subtribe Lathyrinae.
uses: Lathyrus collinus is a wild pea species, and while specific information on its medicinal uses is limited, it is known to have been used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world. The plant contains various compounds that may have potential health benefits, but more research is needed to fully understand its effects. Some studies suggest that Lathyrus species may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant for medicinal purposes. Further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of Lathyrus collinus as a medicinal agent.

preparations: Lathyrus collinus, also known as the rock pea or spring pea, has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. side_effects: Lathyrus collinus is a plant that contains a toxin called ODAP, which can cause serious health issues if ingested in large amounts. Ingestion of the plant can lead to neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness, paralysis, and other neurological problems. Prolonged consumption of the plant can result in irreversible damage to the nervous system. The risk of side effects is particularly high for pregnant women and young children, as their bodies are more susceptible to the toxin. It's essential to exercise caution and avoid consuming Lathyrus collinus altogether to prevent potential harm.
intro: Lathyrus collinus, also known as the woodpeckia pea, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and edible seeds. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus collinus include isoleucine, a non-protein amino acid, as well as other compounds such as saponins and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and supplements in the form of extracts and powders.
